title: Kosuge & Adachi Sightseeing Guide: Your Downtown Base for Skytree and Asakusa
excerpt: A guide to what's within reach of Kosuge, and why it's a smart, affordable base for classic Tokyo sightseeing.
contentHtml:
Kosuge won't appear on most tourist maps, and that's exactly its appeal — a genuine slice of downtown Tokyo life, with the city's biggest sights just a short ride away.

Top Sights

Tokyo Skytree — Japan's tallest structure, with observation decks over the whole city.

Senso-ji Temple & Nakamise-dori — Tokyo's oldest temple and its bustling approach street, a short trip away in Asakusa.

Shiirimachi Park — A riverside park good for a quiet walk.

Arakawa Amusement Park — A small, old-school amusement park, popular with local families.

Nishiarai Daishi Temple — A significant temple with a large, quiet garden.

Food

Asakusa's tempura and dojo restaurants are close by, and Kita-Senju offers a lively strip of izakaya and casual dining favored by locals rather than tourists.

Where to Stay

For an affordable, quiet base within reach of it all, Plat House Kosuge is steps from Kosuge Station, with a full kitchen, Netflix, and Nintendo Switch. Direct booking beats OTA pricing, with free cancellation up to 7 days out.
